commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh If any man trespasses against his neighbor, and an oath is required of him to cause him to swear, and the oath comes before your altar in this house: Oath. In certain cases, an oath would clear a person, Exodus xxii. Solomon prays that perjury may be disclosed. It was customary to touch the altar, when a person took an oath, as Hannibal did. Latinus says; Tango aras mediosque ignes et Numina testor Nulla dies pacem hanc Italis nec foedera rumpet. (Virgil, Æneid xii.
